title: Adopt event bus over direct service calls
body: Engineering team chose to route inter-service messages through a single event bus rather than allowing services to call each other directly.

assumptions.text: Services will rarely need synchronous responses; eventual consistency is acceptable.
assumptions.outcome_evidence: Within six months, three teams introduced ad-hoc HTTP fallbacks because synchronous request/response was load-bearing for the checkout path.

assumptions.text: The event bus team can absorb the operational load of being the central message backbone.

outcome.text: Event bus retained for analytics, replaced by direct gRPC for the checkout path.

title: Switch primary store from PostgreSQL to a wide-column store
body: Migrated the user-profile service primary store from a single PostgreSQL instance to a wide-column store assumed to scale linearly with shard count.

assumptions.text: Linear shard scaling will hold under our hot-key access pattern.
assumptions.outcome_evidence: Hot-key contention pinned 14% of traffic to two shards; linear scaling broke down at the access pattern level even though the storage layer scaled.

title: Standardise on a single feature flag service
body: Mandated that all teams move to one internal feature-flag service to consolidate reporting.

assumptions.text: Per-team flag-management ergonomics are similar enough that one service fits all use cases.
assumptions.outcome_evidence: Most teams adopted within a quarter; ergonomic differences turned out to be cosmetic.
